title: Xolantha glomerata
intro: Xolantha glomerata, also known as Xoconosostle, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Xolantha glomerata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Xoconosostle is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Xolantha glomerata is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification suggests that it shares characteristics with other plants in the Asteraceae family, such as similar flower structures and reproductive patterns. The genus Xolantha is considered to be part of the tribe Mutisieae, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily native to South America. Further classification places Xolantha glomerata as a species within the family Asteraceae, highlighting its unique characteristics among its relatives. The exact classification of Xolantha glomerata is still subject to scientific research and verification.
